utterances.text: Doctor Mehta, Carla, thanks for joining. Before I dive in, I want to confirm I understand your situation correctly.
Yes. We are Nimbus Health, a digital health platform serving hospital systems. Our sales team is sixty-five reps, growing to about a hundred next year.
I'm head of compliance. Before we even talk about features, we need to clear three things: HIPAA, BAA, and data residency.
Absolutely. Cadenza signs a BAA with healthcare customers. We are HIPAA compliant, SOC 2 Type 2, and ISO 27001. I'll send the latest reports as part of follow-up.
Data residency. Our hospital customers require US-only data, and several require it stays inside specific regional hospitals' networks.
We have a US-only data residency option in our enterprise tier. All customer data, including transcripts and embeddings, stays inside our US region. EU is a separate region.
On the workflow side, what we care about is fast outreach to hospital procurement and IT decision-makers. Today our team uses spreadsheets and personal Gmail.
Sequences and personalized cadences are exactly what Cadenza was built for. Your reps can move from Gmail to a structured workflow without losing the personal touch.
I also need to verify the audit trail. For any HIPAA-adjacent action, we need a record of who did what and when, accessible to managers, and ideally exportable for our quarterly audits.
Audit logging is captured today. A self-service UI for admins to browse the log is on the roadmap for this year. Until that ships, we provide it on request through support.
That is acceptable but only short term. The audit log UI is on our must-have list for renewal. Please put that in writing in the next proposal.
Permissions are also crucial. Our regional managers should only see their own team's activity. They cannot see other regions and they certainly cannot see admin-level data.
Out of the box we have a Manager role that is scoped to direct reports plus indirect reports. We can also create custom roles. Carla, you would manage that as an admin.
Good. That's the level of granularity we need. Send the documentation and a proposed permission matrix for our roles.
Let's set up a follow-up call with our procurement team next week. Send slots in the morning Eastern time.
Will do. Thank you both, this was very productive.
